title: AI Video Editor for Ecommerce Ads (Higgsfield, CapCut, ElevenLabs)
description:
We're a fast-growing e-commerce company creating high-converting video ads for DTC brands in supplements, footwear, and more. We're looking for an AI Video Editor who can produce incredibly realistic, AI-generated video ads that look indistinguishable from real footage.



What you'll do:

• Create 20-50 fully AI-generated video ads per week for multiple ecommerce brands

• Produce AI talking heads, UGC-style ads, and b-roll content using advanced AI tools

• Edit and assemble all video content in CapCut with expert-level precision

• Analyze competitor ads on Meta and Google to create high-converting ad variations

• Independently concept and produce creative video ad variations that drive sales

• Assist with scripting when needed (scripts typically provided)



What we're looking for:

• Proven experience creating AI-generated video ads specifically for DTC ecommerce brands

• Strong proficiency with AI video/image generation tools like Higgsfield and ElevenLabs

• Expert-level CapCut editing skills with a portfolio to prove it

• Ability to generate extremely realistic AI visuals of people, products, and environments

• Creative eye for direct-response advertising and what makes ads convert

• Self-starter who can independently produce high-volume ad creatives



Nice to have:

• Experience with Midjourney, Runway, Kling, or Sora

• Familiarity with Meta Ads Library or competitor research tools



This is a part-time position working 32 hours per week with flexible scheduling Monday through Saturday. 



How to Apply:

To make sure you've read this entire post, please start your application with the phrase 'AICreator492'. Then answer the following questions in your application:



1. Walk us through your workflow for creating a fully AI-generated UGC-style video ad from start to finish. From receiving a script to delivering the final edit in CapCut.

2. Which AI tools do you currently use for video generation, image generation, and voice generation? Rate your proficiency with each tool (beginner / intermediate / advanced).

3. How do you ensure that AI-generated talking heads and visuals look realistic enough to be used in paid ads? What techniques or tools do you use to avoid the typical "AI look"?

4. When analyzing competitor ads for inspiration, what specific elements do you look for that indicate high conversion potential?

5. How long does it take you to create a single 25-second short-form video ad that is fully AI-generated, from start to finished edit?

6. Please share your portfolio of extremely realistic AI-generated ecommerce video ads that demonstrate your ability to create converting content.

title: Project Manager
description:
About ElasticIT



At ElasticIT, we're building a world where healthcare workers never wait for technology. We're not just another IT support company; we're the team that rescues healthcare providers when technology threatens patient care, turning chaos into clarity and empowering ordinary teams to achieve extraordinary results through the right technology.



We specialise in healthcare IT, providing managed services, custom software development, and HIPAA-compliant solutions that ensure medical professionals can focus on what matters most: their patients, not their systems. Our North Star is audacious yet simple: by 2050, we will eliminate repetitive IT tasks for 1 million healthcare workers, proving that the right technology makes ordinary people extraordinary.



Our Values: The DNA of How We Operate



Our values aren't just words on a wall; they're the blueprint for every interaction, every solution, and every relationship we build:

•SIMPLIFY THE OVERWHELMING: Cut through confusion with clarity and convert half-hour tasks into one-click wins

•RESCUE WHEN OTHERS PANIC: Respond when others would panic and deliver the impossible under pressure

•BUILD LASTING CAPABILITY: Create tools that spread like wildfire and teams that work without micromanaging

•PERSIST THROUGH CHAOS: Adapt when goalposts keep moving and turn disasters into relationships

•ENABLE HUMAN PURPOSE: Remove barriers so the focus can be on patients, not systems



The Problem We're Solving



Whether it's a client transformation project, an internal ElasticIT initiative, or managing the daily flow of incoming support requests, the same coordination challenges have led to real-world problems:

•Helpdesk teams not informed of go-live dates until the morning of deployment

•Missed deadlines due to lack of structured project planning

•Leadership stretched between strategic client work and operational coordination

•No clear ownership for project tracking and execution accountability

•Internal improvement projects losing 
ntum without dedicated coordination

•Tickets flooding in with no clear priority, leaving critical issues buried amongst routine requests

•Healthcare practices waiting whilst technicians figure out who should handle what

•Urgent issues escalating because initial triage missed the severity



The Role

You'll be the person who turns vague briefs into bulletproof plans and ensures nothing falls through the cracks. When leadership identifies an opportunity, whether that's a client transformation or an internal initiative, you're the one who evaluates what's actually needed, spots the gaps others miss, and uses your judgement to assess risks before they become problems. You'll convert ideas into proper project plans with scheduled tasks, clear ownership, and timely communication to all stakeholders.



This role also carries a secondary responsibility: providing backup and cover for our dispatcher function, managing the flow of incoming tickets when needed. This isn't just about covering holidays, it's about understanding the full lifecycle of our client journey, from the 
nt a ticket arrives through to project completion. By bridging both worlds, you'll develop an invaluable perspective on how our service delivery truly works, seeing the immediate client needs alongside the strategic improvements that prevent those same issues from recurring.



This isn't about being the person who comes up with the big ideas or the technical expert who fixes the problems, it's about being the thoughtful evaluator who instinctively knows what's going to work and the dependable force who ensures nothing falls through the cracks. You're the person who prevents the "four hours late" scenarios because you spotted the gap in the plan early, who follows up when others forget, and who takes genuine satisfaction in crossing tasks off the list. When you say something will be done right, it gets done right.



For client projects, you'll support our vCIO function and technical teams to ensure seamless delivery. For internal projects, you'll work alongside leadership and department heads to drive ElasticIT's own improvements forward. For dispatcher cover, you'll be the calm voice that reassures a panicking practice manager that help is on the way, the eyes that spot a P1 hiding in a sea of password resets, and the hands that keep our ticket queue flowing smoothly. In all cases, you're the person who instinctively knows what's going to work and what isn't, evaluating plans, providing steady coordination, and removing blockers so everyone else can do their best work.



What You'll Do

Primary Function: Project Management

•Translate strategy to execution: Receive strategic briefs from vCIO conversations (for client projects) or leadership discussions (for internal initiatives) and break them down into actionable project phases and milestones

•Create comprehensive project plans: Document objectives, scope, stakeholders, milestones, tasks, dependencies, risks, and communication plans for every project

•Coordinate across teams: Brief technical teams before go-lives, consult with TAM on technical considerations, ensure clients and internal stakeholders receive proactive updates

•Drive accountability: Follow up on tasks, remove blockers, escalate issues early, and ensure deadlines are met or communicated in advance

•Maintain documentation: Keep project records, capture lessons learned, and drive process improvements

•Drive internal initiatives: Coordinate ElasticIT's own improvement projects, from process automation to strategic business development activities, ensuring internal work gets the same rigour as client projects

•Enable leadership focus: Free vCIO and leadership to focus on strategic work without operational distractions



Secondary Function: Dispatcher/Triage Backup

•Triage incoming tickets: Rapidly assess, categorise, and prioritise all incoming requests during cover periods, ensuring nothing critical gets lost in the noise

•Provide first response: Acknowledge every ticket promptly, gather essential information, and set client expectations clearly

•Route intelligently: Match tickets to the right technician based on skill, availability, and urgency – not just whoever's next

•Escalate appropriately: Recognise P1 and P2 situations immediately and trigger the right response protocols

•Monitor queue health: Keep an eye on ticket age, SLA breaches, and workload distribution, flagging issues before they become problems

•Support client communication: Keep clients informed of progress, especially during high-priority i



About You

Essential:

•Strong project management fundamentals, planning, scheduling, tracking

•Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal

•Highly organised with exceptional attention to detail

•Ability to ask the right questions and drive clarity

•Ability to stay calm under pressure and maintain clarity when things get busy

•Basic technical understanding, enough to distinguish a network outage from a forgotten password

•Empathy and patience, healthcare workers calling us are often stressed and need reassurance



Nice to Have:

•Experience with PSA tools (we use Halo but experience with any PSA is helpful)

•Healthcare IT background

•Technical certifications

•Understanding of MSP landscape and terminology (can be taught)

•Background in IT support or helpdesk environments



Not Essential:

Deep technical skills. This role doesn't fix tickets; it coordinates people who do. A non-technical person with great organisational skills and a calm demeanour is often better than a technician trying to be organised.



Who you are:

•Someone with a natural instinct for what's going to work, you can look at a plan and immediately sense what's missing or what's about to go wrong

•Able to take a vague brief and quickly evaluate what actually needs to happen, you turn ambiguity into clarity without needing everything spelled out

•Finds genuine satisfaction in completing what you start, an unfinished task list keeps you up at night, and you get real energy from crossing things off as done

•Takes pride in being the person others trust for good judgement, when you say something will work, people believe you because you're usually right

•Gets energy from helping others succeed, whether that's spotting a problem before it derails the team, providing support when someone's stuck, or being the reassuring voice when a client is stressed

•Thrives with clear direction, you don't need to invent the strategy or ask "why are we doing this?", you need to know what success looks like so you can evaluate the best path to get there

•Committed to follow-through, once you've assessed what needs doing and said yes, you're determined to deliver, no matter how tedious the final 10?comes

•Someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, you don't freeze when the pace picks up, you focus

•Naturally empathetic, when a stressed practice manager calls, you genuinely want to help them feel better



What We Offer

•Work that matters: Every project you see through to completion and every ticket you triage correctly directly impacts healthcare delivery and patient care, your follow-through makes a real difference

•Clear direction and support: Leadership who sets clear objectives and then trusts you to deliver, with documented procedures and systematic approaches that reward thorough, careful work

Growth potential: Develop into senior project management, operations leadership, or client success roles

•Recognition for reliability: Work alongside people who genuinely value dependability and follow-through, where being the person who gets things done is celebrated, not taken for granted

•Purpose you can commit to: Contribute directly to our North Star of liberating 1 million healthcare workers from repetitive IT tasks, a mission worth seeing through



THE PRACTICAL STUFF

POSITION: Full-time (40 hours/week), remote

HOURS: Mostly U.S. Eastern Time (8 AM - 5 PM EST) with some flexibility

- Full professional attention must be dedicated to EIT during scheduled working hours — no freelance, consulting, or secondary employment during shift.



READY TO APPLY?

Complete our application form: 
/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=mSS-jteCcUa2SUvZ2Pn71DLj3g8jt9NFjsUkZjVNOzBUOTRQVzRGWjFURTZNSjM2TjFQVlFHWDhYUC4u



WHAT WE WANT TO SEE:

A short video introducing yourself - (1-2 minutes)

Filled out form - 
/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=mSS-jteCcUa2SUvZ2Pn71DLj3g8jt9NFjsUkZjVNOzBUOTRQVzRGWjFURTZNSjM2TjFQVlFHWDhYUC4u

Your experience with examples of managing competing priorities

Why you think you'd be good at this type of controlled chaos

title: Experienced Video Editor (DTC eCommerce)
description:
About Us

We are a fast-growing health and wellness brand obsessed with customer psychology and creative excellence. We don't just "make content," we build high-performance assets that drive millions in revenue. We are looking for a Video Editor who is genuinely passionate about performance, speed, and continuous improvement.



This is not a role for someone who just wants to follow a storyboard and clock out. We need a creative who looks at a hook and immediately thinks of three ways to make it more engaging, faster, and higher-converting.



Our philosophy: we prioritize 10 ads at 80% quality over 2 ads at 100%. Speed and volume win in performance marketing. If you're someone who obsesses over pixel-perfect polish at the expense of output, this isn't the right fit.



The Role

You will own the creation of high-volume, high-performance video ads, targeting 10–20 videos per week depending on length. This includes short-form for Meta/TikTok, long-form VSLs, AI avatars, B-roll edits, and voiceover-driven content.



You'll work from creative briefs and scripts provided by our creative strategists and copywriters, turning direction into polished, high-converting assets at speed. You take deep pride in ownership, turn raw footage and AI outputs into systematic creative machines, and ensure our creative output is a competitive advantage.



If you are a builder who thrives on iterating based on data rather than just "vibes," we want to talk to you.



What You Will Do

* Full-Spectrum Production: Create high-converting short-form ads (15–60s), UGC-style content, and long-form VSLs for Meta, TikTok, and YouTube.

* AI Mastery: Take full ownership of our AI tech stack, including Veo 3, HeyGen, Runway, and ElevenLabs for video, and Nano-Banana for statics, to produce high-quality AI video, avatar, and voiceover outputs at scale.

* Source Fresh Creatives: Proactively find and source fresh B-roll and visual assets from across the internet. You never run dry, and you never recycle.



What We're Looking For

Experience & Skills

* 2+ years of video editing experience, with specific experience editing for DTC brands or performance marketing.

* Technical mastery: Fluency in Premiere, Final Cut, or CapCut, plus advanced proficiency in AI video tools (Veo 3, HeyGen, Runway, ElevenLabs, Nano-Banana).

* Strategic thinking: Strong instincts for hooks, pacing, and storytelling in a Direct Response (DR) context.

* Resourcefulness: Proven ability to source fresh creatives, B-roll, and visual assets independently. You always have new material to work with.



Work Style

* Speed over perfection. You can move fast and deliver high volume without sacrificing the quality that matters.

* Implement feedback quickly, often same-day turnaround.

* Improvement-driven: You can demonstrate how you've personally improved a creative's performance through iteration or better editing workflows.

* Excellent English, clear written and verbal communication for seamless collaboration.

* Self-learner. You figure things out on your own. Whether it's a new tool, a new technique, or a new platform, you don't wait to be taught.

* Organized file structures and version control for rapid iteration.



Commitment

Full-time and exclusive. This role requires your full professional focus. We invest heavily in our people, training, tools, growth, and we're looking for someone who's equally invested in building something long-term with us.



Click here to apply :

title: Children's Book Layout & KDP Publishing Assistant
description:
THE ROLE



I'm a children's book author with an active production pipeline of 3+ books ready for layout and publishing. I need a detail-obsessed VA who can take locked illustration files and manuscript text, build press-ready layouts in Canva, and upload finished books to Amazon KDP.



This is NOT a creative role. The design is already done. Your job is precision assembly and technical execution — like a machinist, not an artist. If you love following exact specs and delivering clean, error-free output, this role is for you.



This is a long-term, recurring role. We have a 17-book series in production. You are joining a system, not completing a one-time project.



REQUIREMENTS

• Canva (intermediate–advanced) — REQUIRED

• Amazon KDP upload experience — REQUIRED

• Strong attention to detail and spec-following — NON-NEGOTIABLE

• Reliable internet, consistent availability, clean file management

• English reading comprehension — must place text accurately per manuscript

• Adobe InDesign experience — BONUS (not required)



YOU ARE NOT RIGHT FOR THIS ROLE IF...

• You make creative decisions without instruction

• You deliver files without checking against specs

• You disappear mid-project without communication

• You 'figure it out' on the illustration itself instead of flagging the issue





WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Resize illustration files to 8.75 × 8.75 inches at 300 DPI with 0.125" bleed

• Build 40-page children's book layouts in Canva (8.5 × 8.5 trim size)

• Place manuscript text over illustrations per exact page specs

• Make minor Canva-level page adjustments — reposition text, adjust overlays, fix spacing, correct text band placement so it does not cover key visual elements

• Export press-quality PDFs (PDF Print setting, embedded fonts)

• Upload files to Amazon KDP (title, description, keywords, categories)

• Order and verify digital proof before publishing approval

• Flag any issue you cannot resolve at the Canva level — do NOT improvise solutions on the illustration itself

SCOPE LINE: Canva-level page adjustments are IN scope. Illustration editing (changing what is inside the art) is NOT in scope and will never be asked of you.   APPLICATION FILTER TEST

To be considered for this role, complete the following short test. Do NOT skip it. Applications without the completed test will not be reviewed.



TASK 1

What is the final canvas size (in inches) you would use in Canva to build an 8.5 × 8.5 inch book with 0.125" bleed on all sides?

TASK 2

In Canva, what export setting would you use for a press-ready print file — PDF Standard or PDF Print — and why?

TASK 3

Name one specific thing you would check in the KDP digital previewer before approving a book for sale.

TASK 4 — Canva Adjustment Test

Open Canva. Create a new design at 8.75 × 8.75 inches. Place any image on the canvas. Add a translucent text band at the bottom. For Task 4, upload your screenshot to Google Drive and paste the shareable link in your application.



TASK 5 — Scope Judgment

You are laying out a page and notice the text band is covering the character's face. What do you do? What would be in scope for you to fix, and what would you flag to the author?

description:
About the Role:

You’ll play a key role in evolving our proprietary platforms, working closely with cross-functional teams to deliver sophisticated functionality across both internal tools and customer-facing services.

title: Software Quality Assurance (SQA) Analyst
description:
# Software Quality Assurance (SQA) Analyst



## About Us

We are a software consultancy specializing in custom web and mobile application development for small and medium-sized businesses. We pride ourselves on delivering high-quality, tailored solutions that help our clients streamline their operations and grow their businesses.



## Position Overview

We're seeking a detail-oriented SQA Analyst to join our team and ensure the quality and reliability of the custom applications we develop for our clients. You'll work closely with our development team to identify issues, validate functionality, and help us maintain the high standards our clients expect.



## Key Responsibilities

- Design, develop, and execute comprehensive test plans and test cases for web and mobile applications

- Perform functional, regression, integration, and user acceptance testing across multiple projects

- Document and track defects in Jira, providing clear reproduction steps and supporting evidence

- Collaborate with developers and project managers to understand requirements and acceptance criteria

- Verify bug fixes and validate that applications meet client specifications

- Conduct exploratory testing to identify edge cases and potential user experience issues

- Participate in sprint planning and retrospectives, providing QA perspective on story estimates

- Create and maintain testing documentation including test scenarios, test data, and QA reports

- Test applications across different browsers, devices, and operating systems

- Contribute to continuous improvement of our QA processes and standards



## Required Qualifications

- 2+ years of experience in software quality assurance or testing

- Strong understanding of QA methodologies, testing types, and best practices

- Hands-on experience with Jira for defect tracking and project management

- Experience testing both web and mobile applications

- **Basic experience with automated testing tools and frameworks**

- **Proven manual testing experience**

- Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with strong attention to detail

- Ability to clearly document issues and communicate with technical and non-technical stakeholders

- Self-motivated with ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously

- Understanding of the software development lifecycle and agile methodologies



## Preferred Qualifications

- Experience with advanced test automation frameworks (Selenium, Cypress, Appium, etc.)

- Familiarity with API testing tools (Postman, REST clients)

- Basic understanding of SQL for database validation

- Knowledge of web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript)

- Experience with mobile testing on iOS and Android platforms

- ISTQB or similar QA certification



## What We Offer

- Opportunity to work on diverse projects across multiple industries

- Collaborative team environment where your input is valued

- Flexible work arrangements

- Professional development opportunities



## How to Apply

To apply for this position, please submit:



1. **Your resume** in PDF format

2. **A video introduction** (less than 3 minutes) where you appear on screen the entire time explaining:

   - Your professional background in software testing

   - Your experience with both manual and automated testing

   - Why you would be a good fit for this position

   - What interests you about working with a consultancy that serves SMBs



We look forward to learning more about you!

title: Do you Speak Spanish? Apply Here
description:
Description: This position has primary responsibility for staffing and scheduling services for Assisting Hands®. The Staffing Coordinator matches caregivers with clients and coordinates caregiver schedules. This position involves extensive telephone work and interaction with caregivers, clients, and health care professionals. The Staffing Coordinator also responds to inquiries from prospective clients. 



Qualifications:  Excellent telephone and interpersonal skills in English and Spanish, must be well-organized and have good attention to detail. Requires at least two years experience in staffing or customer service in a health care setting.  Experience with home care, home health, or senior services preferred. Must be a good problem solver and able to work independently. 



Reports to: Office Manager and Administrator



Primary Duties:

1. Staff cases – matches appropriate caregivers to jobs that are called in by clients, case managers and others.

2. Makes sure that all cases are covered. Arrange for substitute/back-up coverage as necessary.

3. Assists with answering phones.

4. Handles/resolves client and caregiver issues, problems and scheduling changes.

5. Provides information about services to prospective clients.

6. Responsible for maintaining client files and keeping them up to date.

7. Responsible for verifying authorization of services with payors.

8. Serves as the point of contact for clients, caregivers and case managers.

9. Counsels caregivers regarding attendance or performance issues. Make recommendations to Administrator regarding the termination of caregivers. 

10. Works closely with the bookkeeper to ensure that hours and pay rates are correct for payroll.

11. Handles after-hours on-call services as needed. This means answering the phone after 5:00pm (Monday – Friday). Also, during the weekend, be available to address any emergencies.

12. Must have a caring personality and be willing to support other functions of the administrative team as needed.



Hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (Miami), Monday through Friday and on call availability

title: Video Editor & Graphic Designer for Meta Ads
description:
We’re a performance-focused ecommerce team scaling through systematic creative testing on Meta.



We don’t care about “pretty.” We care about what converts.



We’re looking for a video editor with strong graphic design skills who understands direct response marketing and performance-driven creatives.



This is not a brand aesthetics role. This is about results.



For the right fit, this can turn into a full-time position.



What You’ll Do



? Edit direct response video ads for Meta

? Create multiple hook variations from one winning concept

? Turn video winners into static derivatives

? Design performance-focused static ads

? Iterate quickly based on data

? Work inside a structured creative testing system



We test aggressively. We move fast. We scale what works.



What We Need From You



? Strong understanding of direct response principles

? You understand hooks, benefits, proof, and urgency

? You can break down competitor ads and identify what makes them work

? You can produce high-volume variations weekly

? Fast turnaround time

? Comfortable working with feedback and performance metrics



Preferred Tools



? Premiere, CapCut, or Final Cut

? Photoshop or Figma

? Canva for rapid iterations



You’re NOT a Fit If



? You focus on aesthetics over performance

? You need hand-holding on every variation

? You take multiple days to finish 1 ad

? You get attached to creatives and resist iteration

? You don’t understand scroll behavior on Meta



Growth Opportunity



This role starts performance-focused. For the right person, this will evolve into a full-time position with larger creative ownership and scaling responsibility across campaigns.



To Apply



Send:



Portfolio with direct response ads only



2–3 examples where you created multiple variations from one concept



Include the word potato in your
 subject line so we know you read the full job post.



If you’re serious about performance and want to grow with a structured ecommerce team, apply.

title: Job Title: GHL / Skool  Funnel & Digital Operations Specialist
description:
DR. JOHN R. BAIRD, MD

GHL Funnel & Digital Operations Specialist

Job Description & Performance Standards

 

ROLE OVERVIEW

 

This is a high-output, execution-focused role supporting a fast-growing functional medicine education business. You will be responsible for building, maintaining, and optimizing GoHighLevel (GHL) funnels, landing pages,
 sequences, and digital assets using pre-built templates, HTML files, and detailed build guides provided to you.

 

This is not a design or copywriting role. All content, copy, and design direction is provided. Your job is fast, accurate execution with zero ambiguity.

 

POSITION DETAILS

 

Job Title

GHL Funnel & Digital Operations Specialist

Type

Part-Time Contract / Hourly

Hours

5–15 hours per week (project-based)

Rate

Negotiable — based on deliverables, not open-ended hourly billing

Location

Remote — must overlap with EST business hours

Start

Immediate

 

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 

GoHighLevel Build & Management

• Build landing pages from provided HTML files and written build guides

• Set up funnels including landing page ? thank you page ? application form ? booking

• Configure
 sequences and automation workflows in GHL

• Integrate calendar/booking links and payment forms

• QA all pages for mobile responsiveness and broken links before submission

• Maintain and update existing pages as directed

 

Digital Asset Management

• Upload, organize, and publish course content in GHL or Skool as directed

• Format and upload PDFs, slide decks, and reference documents to member portals

• Maintain organized file structure in Google Drive

• Resize and optimize images for web use

 & Communication Sequences

• Build
 sequences from provided scripts — no copywriting required

• Set up triggers, tags, and automation logic in GHL

• Test all sequences before going live

 

Reporting & Communication

• Submit a time log with every invoice — task, date, and hours broken down

• Flag blockers within 2 hours — do not spin on a problem for a day

• Provide a completion report with screenshots when each deliverable is done

• Respond to messages within 4 hours during agreed working hours

 

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

 

• GoHighLevel (GHL) — minimum 6 months hands-on experience

• Ability to build GHL pages from an HTML reference file

• Basic HTML/CSS — enough to copy, paste, and troubleshoot layout issues

• Google Drive, Docs, Sheets — intermediate level

• Skool platform experience — preferred but not required

• Attention to detail — pixel-accurate execution against provided specs

• Fast turnaround — standard deliverables completed within 24–48 hours

 

DELIVERABLE TIME STANDARDS

 

All work is benchmarked. Invoices that exceed these standards require a written explanation. These benchmarks assume all content, copy, and design direction is provided upfront.

 

DELIVERABLE

MAX HOURS

NOTES

Full landing page (from HTML file)

4–5 hrs

HTML + build guide provided

Thank you page

45 min

Template-based

Application / opt-in form

1 hr

Fields specified in brief

 sequence (3–5
s)

2–3 hrs

Scripts provided

Calendar / booking integration

1 hr

Link + settings provided

GHL pipeline setup

1 hr

Stages defined in brief

Complete funnel (all above)

10–13 hrs

All assets provided upfront

PDF upload + portal formatting

30 min

Per document

Mobile QA + bug fixes

1 hr

Per funnel

Skool course upload (per module)

45 min

Content provided

 

NON-NEGOTIABLES — READ BEFORE APPLYING

APPLYING

 

• You must submit a detailed time log with every invoice. No log = no payment.

• You will not bill for learning GHL, watching tutorials, or figuring out basic platform functions.

• If a task is taking longer than the benchmark, you flag it immediately — you do not continue billing.

• All deliverables require a completion screenshot or Loom walkthrough before payment is released.

• Trial project required before ongoing engagement is offered.

 

WHAT IS PROVIDED TO YOU

 

You will never be asked to write copy, design pages, or make creative decisions. Everything below is provided before each task begins:

 

• Complete HTML file for every landing page

• Written step-by-step GHL build guide

• All copy, headlines, and body text in a content document

• Color codes, font names, and spacing specifications

•
 scripts ready to paste

• All images, PDFs, and assets pre-formatted

 

HOW TO APPLY

 

To be considered, your application must include all of the following:

 

1. A brief description of your GHL experience (platforms built, funnels completed)

2. 2–3 screenshots or links to GHL funnels or pages you have built

3. Your hourly rate or preferred per-deliverable rate

4. Your typical turnaround time for a full landing page build

5. Confirmation that you can submit detailed time logs with each invoice

 

Send applications to: 

Subject line: GHL Specialist — [Your Name]

 

Dr. John R. Baird, MD

Board-Certified Anti-Aging, Functional & Regenerative Medicine · Louisville, KY

title: Editorial Assistant / Journalist
description:
We are a leading tech news publisher seeking two highly reliable and detail-obsessed Editorial Assistants to join our newsroom. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with a journalism background or editorial experience who thrives in a fast-paced, accuracy-driven environment.



As an Editorial Assistant, you will play a key supporting role in our editorial operations, ensuring that content flows smoothly through our news production process each day. Your work will directly support our editors and reporters, helping maintain the high standards our readers expect.



Your tasks will include managing editorial workflows and daily publishing operations, assisting with news writing, fact-checking, and copy polishing, monitoring competitor coverage, and managing our Newsdesk system 
 triage, spam marking, duplicate handling, approvals, etc.). You will also handle basic image editing and support our social media engagement efforts.



This is not a generic admin role. It requires a sharp editorial eye, outstanding written English, and a deep respect for accuracy and detail.



We are looking for someone who has:



* A journalism degree or experience in a newsroom, publication, or editorial role

* Flawless English writing and editing skills (of sub-editor quality)

* A keen eye for detail, grammar, and factual accuracy

* The ability to work independently, anticipate needs, and show initiative

* Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure



Hours are flexibly but ideally 8am to 4:30pm Phillipines time.



To apply:

Write a short, thoughtful cover letter introducing yourself and explaining why you’d be an excellent fit for our newsroom.



*** Applications without a relevant cover letter will not be considered. ***

title: Senior B2B Outbound SDR – UK/EU Fleets & International Distributor Development
description:
Senior B2B Outbound SDR – UK, Europe & International Distributor Development



Full-Time | Performance-Based Growth Role



About ShoulderSink



ShoulderSink is a European manufacturer of professional mobile hand hygiene systems used primarily in commercial vehicles. More than 50,000 professionals use our systems across multiple markets. We are a small team and operate strictly B2B and work with fleet customers and national distributors.



We are expanding across the UK, Europe and selected international markets, including relevant markets such as US and Japan.



The Role

This is a performance-driven outbound role focused on building qualified pipeline in:



- UK fleet market

- European fleet customers

- International distributor markets (including US, Japan and other relevant regions)



This is a full-time role designed for long-term growth. Continued development and expanded responsibility are performance-based.



What You Will Do

- Fleet Development (UK & Europe)

-Cold outreach to fleet managers, operations directors and HSE managers

Ad Ripping/Clearing: I provide the links/footage. You "clear" the video, add natural-sounding AI voiceovers (we provide tools), and add localized subtitles.

title: Mental Health Billing Specialist
description:
Mental Health Billing Specialist



Organization: Wings of the Future

Location: [Remote]

Job Type: Full-Time / Part-Time / Contract



About Wings of the Future



Wings of the Future is a trauma-informed organization dedicated to helping individuals and families heal from trauma and develop healthy, functional relationships. Our services include mental health counseling, trauma-informed coaching, and psychoeducation designed to help clients move from survival to healing and thriving.



We are seeking a detail-oriented Mental Health Billing Specialist to manage insurance claims, payment processing, and billing compliance for our behavioral health services.



Position Summary



The Mental Health Billing Specialist is responsible for managing the full medical billing cycle for behavioral health services, including insurance verification, claim submission, payment posting, and resolving claim denials. This role ensures that all billing processes follow insurance guidelines, Medicaid regulations, and HIPAA compliance standards.



The ideal candidate has experience billing for mental health and behavioral health services and is familiar with major commercial insurance and government payers.



Insurance Panels / Payers



The Billing Specialist will manage billing and claims for the following insurance providers:



• Medicaid

• Medicare

• Blue Cross Blue Shield (BCBS)

• Aetna

• Cigna

*Healthnet

• United Healthcare / Optum

• Tricare

• Beacon Health Options / Carelon Behavioral Health

• Molina Healthcare

• Superior HealthPlan

• Community Health Choice

• Ambetter

• Other commercial and managed care plans as contracted.



Key Responsibilities

Insurance Billing & Claims Management



• Submit behavioral health claims electronically to insurance providers

• Review and process CPT codes for mental health services (ex: 90791, 90834, 90837, 90847, etc.)

• Monitor claim status and follow up on unpaid or rejected claims

• Correct and resubmit denied or rejected claims



Insurance Verification



• Verify insurance eligibility and benefits before services

• Confir
ntal health coverage, copays, deductibles, and authorizations

• Ensure correct payer information is entered in the billing system



Payment Processing



• Post insurance payments and patient payments

• Reconcile Explanation of Benefits (EOBs)

• Process patient statements and balances



Accounts Receivable Management



• Track outstanding claims and follow up on aging accounts

• Communicate with insurance companies to resolve payment issues

• Manage billing disputes and appeals



Compliance & Documentation



• Maintain compliance with HIPAA regulations and billing guidelines

• Ensure documentation supports billed services

• Monitor coding accuracy for behavioral health services



Collaboration



• Work with clinicians to correct documentation errors when necessary

• Communicate with administrative staff regarding billing updates

• Support leadership with billing reports and revenue tracking



Qualifications



Required



• Experience in mental health or behavioral health billing

• Knowledge of insurance billing processes and claim management

• Familiarity with Medicaid and commercial insurance billing

• Understanding of CPT, ICD-10, and behavioral health coding

• Experience with EHR / billing software systems

• Strong attention to detail and organization



Preferred



• 2+ years of medical billing experience in mental health

• Experience with credentialing and insurance authorizations

• Knowledge of Texas Medicaid billing guidelines



Skills



• Insurance claim management

• Medical billing and coding

• Denial management and appeals

• Accounts receivable tracking

• HIPAA compliance

• Attention to detail

• Strong communication skills



Compensation



$5 – $8 per hour (based on experience)



How to Apply



Submit your resume and billing experience to:



Wings of the Future

:

 Bootstrap

* Likes to push their sleeves up to find a needle in a haystack

description:
The Opportunity

We are looking for two talented graphic designers who can produce beautiful, branded content at high volume. Our content strategist handles all the copy and direction — your job is to take that and turn it into scroll-stopping visuals, fast.

This is a contractor / part-time remote position with room to grow as our client roster expands.



What You’ll Create

Here is the current weekly output required across our client base:

 • Instagram &
 Carousels — 5 to 7 slides each, hyperlocal real estate themes, 3 per client per week

 • Reels — 4 per client per week (editing and personalizing templated video reels)

 • Google Business Profile posts — branded, on-template

 • Social media graphics across Instagram,
, and LinkedIn

 • YouTube thumbnails

 • Occasional lead magnet graphics and digital branding assets



At 8 clients today, that is approximately 168 carousel slides and 32 reels per week. At 30 clients, the volume triples. If that excites you, keep reading.



What We’re Looking For

 • Strong Canva skills (this is our primary design tool — you must know it inside and out)

 • Experience designing social media content, especially carousels and Instagram posts

 • Ability to edit and personalize video reels from templates (a big bonus)

 • Fast turnaround — we operate at speed and need reliable daily output

 • Strong attention to brand consistency and design guidelines

 • Real estate or lifestyle/local content experience is a plus, not required

 • Excellent communication and ability to work independently

 • Portfolio or samples of past social media design work required



What We Offer

 • $4 – $5 per hour, paid based on output and quality

 • Fully remote — work from anywhere on your schedule

 • Consistent, ongoing work with a growing client base

 • Clear design guidelines and templates provided — no guessing

 • Content and copy are provided to you — you focus on design

 • Direct onboarding support from our team

 • Opportunity to grow into a long-term, higher-volume role as we scale



A Note From Us

We hire fast, onboard thoroughly, and give you every tool you need to succeed. We’re not looking for perfect — we’re looking for talented, reliable, and hungry. If your design work is great and you show up consistently, there is serious growth here.

We are hiring 2 designers right now so we can move at pace and find the best fit through real output. Give us your best work and the rest will take care of itself.

title: Product/Campaign Lister
description:
WE'RE HIRING — Products & Campaign Lister

E-Commerce / Dropshipping | Remote | Hours TBD | Market-Rate Pay



About Us

We run fast-growing e-commerce brands built on Shopify, selling to customers across the US. Our team is small, fully remote, and moves fast. We test new products constantly, launch campaigns daily, and need someone who can keep up with both someone systematic, detail-oriented, and execution-focused.

Right now, product listing and campaign setup is handled by the founders. We need someone who is sharper and faster at this than we are, and who takes full ownership from day one.



The Role

You will own two core functions: finding and listing winning products on our Shopify stores, and setting up
 ad campaigns correctly from brief to live. Copy and creatives are provided by the team your job is precise, fast, and consistent execution across multiple stores and ad accounts. Scaling and optimisation stays with us.



What you'll do day-to-day:



Upload products to Shopify: titles, descriptions (copy provided), images, variants, and pricing — accurately.

Ensure every listing is complete and ready to sell before going live

Set up
 ad campaigns in Ads Manager from scratch based on team briefs

Upload creatives (video and image) correctly, naming conventions, placements, and formats must be exact

Configure audiences, budgets, and campaign structures as briefed

Double-check every campaign before launch: no missing creatives, wrong budgets, or targeting errors

Maintain a product pipeline and campaign setup log across all active stores and accounts

Flag issues with briefs, supplier pages, or ad account compliance before they cause delays

Build and maintain listing and campaign launch checklists we can scale with





Who We're Looking For

We need a medior-level operator. Someone who has done both of these things before, works independently, and doesn't need hand-holding on every upload or campaign build. You are fast, accurate, and detail-obsessed — sloppy listings and policy flags are not in your vocabulary.



Must-haves:



At least 1 year of hands-on experience listing products on Shopify

At least 1 year of hands-on experience setting up
 ad campaigns in Ads Manager

Proven experience researching winning products for dropshipping stores (US market)

Strong understanding of
 campaign structure and ad policy compliance rules

Experience uploading and managing creatives across multiple ad accounts

Sharp eye for detail,  wrong prices, missing variants, broken images, or budget errors never go live

Able to work from a brief independently, without needing strategy explained every time

Comfortable managing multiple stores and ad accounts simultaneously

Self-directed and reliable in a fully remote, async environment



Nice-to-haves:



Familiar with product research tools (Minea, AdSpy, or similar)

Basic understanding of SEO fields on Shopify product pages

Experience with
 Pixel setup and basic event verification

Experience building listing or campaign launch SOPs from scratch





Compensation

Pay is market-rate and based on experience. Hours are flexible and agreed upon together — we care about output, not clock-watching. We are open to discussing a per-task rate or a fixed part-time rate depending on volume. Quarterly rate reviews for people who grow with us.



How to Apply:

We filter carefully. Generic applications will not be considered. Show us you've done both sides of this role and that you want this specific position.

Send us a whatsapp on 
with a document with the subject line:

"Lister Application – [Your Name] – Shopify +
"

Your whatsapp must include the following — no more, no less:



A short paragraph (5-8 sentences) on your most relevant product listing and campaign experience

One
 campaign you set up — describe the structure and what you were working from

A screenshot of a Shopify listing AND a campaign structure you built (attach as PDF or image)

Your availability and preferred rate structure (hourly / per task / fixed part-time)



Applications that don't follow this format will not receive a reply. We respect your time and ask you to respect ours.

title: SEO Stragist for Growing Law Firm Webiste
description:
We are looking for an experienced **SEO Strategist** to help improve the visibility, traffic, and lead generation of our law firm website. We want someone who understands how to build a strong SEO foundation, identify opportunities for growth, and create a practical strategy that helps our firm rank for the right keywords and attract qualified leads.



This is not just a one-time audit role. We are looking for someone who can evaluate our current website, identify what needs to be improved, and help implement an ongoing SEO strategy.



**About Us**

We are a growing law firm focused on serving clients with excellence and building a strong online presence. We want our website to rank well, reflect our brand, and convert visitors into consultations.



**What We Need Help With**



* Conducting a full SEO audit of our website

* Reviewing on-page SEO, technical SEO, and local SEO performance

* Keyword research for our practice areas and target locations

* Optimizing website pages, metadata, headings, and internal linking

* Creating a content strategy to improve rankings and authority

* Advising on blog topics, service pages, and location pages

* Reviewing site structure and user experience as it relates to SEO

* Identifying opportunities to improve Google rankings and lead generation

* Monitoring performance and providing clear monthly reporting

* Making recommendations based on data, not guesswork



**Ideal Candidate**



* Proven experience as an SEO strategist or SEO specialist

* Strong understanding of technical SEO, on-page SEO, and local SEO

* Experience improving rankings for service-based businesses; law firm experience is a plus

* Ability to perform keyword research and competitor analysis

* Comfortable using SEO tools such as Google Search Console, Google Analytics, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Screaming Frog, or similar

* Able to explain strategy clearly in plain language

* Strong attention to detail and ability to prioritize high-impact tasks

* Experience creating SEO strategies that drive actual leads, not just traffic



**Preferred Qualifications**



* Experience with legal marketing or professional services

* Familiarity with local SEO and Google Business Profile optimization

* Experience working with website platforms such as WordPress

* Understanding of how SEO supports conversion and overall marketing goals



**Scope of Work**

We would like to start with:



1. A website SEO audit

2. A prioritized action plan

3. Recommendations for short-term and long-term improvements

4. Ongoing monthly SEO support if it is a good fit



**To Apply**

Please include:



* A brief summary of your SEO experience

* Examples of websites or businesses you have helped grow

* Tools you use for SEO audits and reporting

* Your approach to improving rankings for a service-based business

* Whether you have experience with law firms or local SEO



We are looking for someone strategic, organized, and results-driven who can help us build a strong long-term SEO presence.



If you are confident in your ability to identify opportunities, improve rankings, and help generate more qualified leads, we would love to hear from you.

title: Urgent hiring process- Appointment Setter
description:
READ THE INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY. Applications that do not follow instructions will NOT be reviewed.



ABOUT US



ZZ Med Spa is a fast growing medical spa company based in Manhattan, New York. We operate multiple Day Spa and Med Spa locations and currently employ over 80 tea
mbers across our organization.



We are expanding our Appointment Setting team and are looking for experienced, confident professionals who are serious about long term work and performance.



ROLE OVERVIEW



As an Appointment Setter, you will be responsible for contacting leads, booking appointments, and maintaining accurate CRM records. This role requires urgency, strong communication skills, and the ability to confidently guide prospects toward scheduling.



This is not an entry level role. We are looking for individuals with real outbound experience who understand follow up discipline and conversion.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



• Conduct high volume outbound calls and SMS outreach to potential customers

• Contact new leads immediately and follow up consistently

• Schedule qualified prospects into our calendars

• Maintain accurate and detailed CRM records

• Handle objections professionally and clearly explain service benefits

• Track daily metrics including call volume, conversion rates, and appointments set



REQUIREMENTS



• Minimum 3 years of experience in appointment setting or outbound sales

• Excellent spoken and written English with clear pronunciation and confident delivery

• Strong persuasion and objection handling skills

• Experience using CRM systems and Microsoft Office

• Organized, disciplined, and goal oriented

• Reliable high speed internet and a professional work environment

• Available full time during New York business hours



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



We are seeking candidates who are confident on the phone, proactive in follow up, and comfortable working in a performance driven environment. You should be resilient, coachable, and motivated by results.



Qualified candidates will be contacted for the next step in our screening process.



We are planning to onboard quickly.





APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS



To be considered, please include the following:

1. The total number of years you have in appointment setting or outbound sales

2. A short voice recording introducing yourself and summarizing your relevant experience

3. Your updated resume

4. Write “RADIANCE” in the subject line of your application so we know you read the full job description.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.

title: CRM Manager
description:
Job Overview

We are seeking a highly organized and analytical CRM Manager to oversee and optimize our Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system for our growing real estate team. The CRM Manager will play a critical role in managing lead flow, ensuring data integrity, supporting the Inside Sales Agent (ISA) team, and driving conversion through automation, reporting, and process optimization.

The ideal candidate understands real estate lead pipelines, sales processes, and CRM automation, and can translate data into actionable insights that improve agent productivity and lead conversion.



Key Responsibilities

CRM System Management 

Manage and maintain the company’s CRM platform - Follow-up Boss





Ensure accurate lead routing, tagging, and pipeline stage management.





Maintain CRM data integrity by auditing duplicate records and incomplete entries.





Customize fields, workflows, pipelines, and automation.





Lead Management & Distribution

Oversee inbound lead flow from multiple sources (portals, ads, website, referrals).





Configure and monitor lead routing rules for agents and ISA teams.





Ensure timely follow-up and accountability for assigned leads.





Track response times and lead conversion performance.





Automation & Campaign Management

Build and manage automated
 and SMS nurture campaigns.





Create drip campaigns for buyer, seller, FSBO, expired, and long-term nurture leads.





Develop workflow automations to streamline follow-ups and tasks.





Sales & ISA Support

Work closely with the Inside Sales Agent (ISA) team and agents to ensure CRM processes support daily outreach.





Monitor lead pipeline activity and appointment setting performance.





Implement systems that improve contact rates and conversion.





Reporting & Analytics

Generate weekly and monthly reports on:





Lead sources





Appointment set vs showed





Conversion rates





Pipeline performance





Create dashboards to track sales KPIs and agent productivity.





Identify opportunities to improve lead conversion and pipeline efficiency.





Process Optimization

Develop CRM SOPs and best practices for agents and staff.





Train agents and tea
mbers on proper CRM usage.





Continuously improve workflows to increase efficiency.

title: Video Editor
description:
I am looking for a highly skilled video editor to help edit a premium real estate education course. This is not a basic talking-head project, and I am not looking for generic social media editing filled with flashy effects, cheesy stock footage, or overused YouTube-style tricks.



The content is built around lifestyle real estate, pre-agent strategy, and blunt, no-BS education for buyers and sellers. I need an editor who can take rough first edits and elevate them with tasteful, intentional visuals that support the points being made on camera.



The style needs to feel modern, elevated, slightly cinematic, data-led, and lifestyle-driven. Think high-end truth bomb + strategic field guide. The finished videos should feel polished and intelligent, not tacky, cluttered, or overproduced.



You must understand how to use visuals to reinforce meaning. That includes clean motion graphics, restrained text overlays, helpful charts and frameworks, and carefully selected b-roll that matches the message being delivered. I do not want random filler footage of smiling couples, handshakes, or luxury drone shots thrown in for no reason.



This is educational content with a strong personal brand voice. The editor should know when to enhance a 
nt and when to let a strong statement breathe.



What I Need



Clean editing of talking-head course videos



Color correction and sound improvement



Tasteful motion graphics and text overlays



Visual support for market data, pricing concepts, timelines, comparisons, and frameworks



Lifestyle and real estate b-roll used with purpose



A consistent visual system across all course modules



Editing Style Requirements



Premium and modern



Slightly cinematic



Blunt and intelligent



Data-led



Lifestyle-driven



Minimal, tasteful motion



Never cheesy



Never cluttered



Never “YouTube bro guru”



Types of Visual Assets to Use



Kinetic statement overlays for key lines and punchy truths



Data-board overlays for market stats, pricing ladders, DOM trends, inventory, buyer pool visuals, and comparisons



Lifestyle cinematic inserts only when they support the point being made



Simple diagrams/frameworks such as timelines, decision trees, psychology charts, and right-way/wrong-way comparisons



What to Avoid



Cheesy real estate stock footage



Fake smiling couples in kitchens



Constant motion for no reason



Overuse of drone footage



Loud transitions and gimmicky effects




-style editing



Overproduced captions flying everywhere



Anything that feels like a sales bro course



Ideal Candidate



Strong portfolio in premium educational or brand content



Good sense of pacing and visual restraint



Can make talking-head videos feel engaging without clutter



Understands business, strategy, or real estate style content



Can follow brand tone and create consistency across a course



Strong English comprehension



Reliable, communicative, and detail-oriented



Nice to Have



Experience with real estate, business education, coaching, or personal brand content



Ability to source and integrate tasteful stock footage and visual assets



Ability to create simple custom branded graphics/templates for recurring use across modules



To Apply



Please send:



3–5 relevant samples of your best work



A short note explaining why your editing style fits this project



What software you use



Your typical turnaround time



Your rate per video or per hour



Screening Questions



Have you edited premium talking-head educational content before?



How do you make a talking-head video more engaging without making it feel cluttered?



How would you approach adding visuals to a lesson about pricing strategy, market shifts, or choosing the right real estate advisor?



What editing mistakes make business or course content feel cheap?



Can you color correct and improve audio as part of your workflow?



Paid Test Project



I may start with one paid test video to find the right fit before assigning the full project.

title: Creative Director (Social Media & Brand Strategy)
description:
Location: Remote

Type: Contract / Part-time (with potential to grow)



Role Overview

We are looking for a Creative Director who will lead the creative strategy for our clients across social

media platforms. This role is responsible for defining the visual and messaging direction of content so

that posts are engaging, trend-aware, and aligned with each client’s brand identity.

The Creative Director will ensure every asset has a strong hook, clear narrative, and strategic purpose,

helping increase engagement, reach, and authority.

This role will also help streamline content approvals by providing expert creative direction that aligns

with client branding and platform best practices.



Key Responsibilities

• Define the creative direction for social media content.

• Ensure every asset has a clear hook, message, and storytelling angle.

• Align content with current social media trends and formats.

• Conduct brand discovery calls with clients.

• Translate client vision into clear brand guidelines.

• Identify tone, style, voice, and messaging framework.

• Guide content creators on hooks, storytelling, visual style, and messaging.

• Review and approve assets before publishing.

• Identify effective content formats and adapt them for our clients.

• Maintain consistency across posts, videos, carousels, and captions.



Platforms

•

• Instagram

• YouTube

• LinkedIn



Requirements

• Proven experience as a Creative Director, Content Strategist, or similar role.

• Strong understanding of social media hooks and engaging content.

• Experience in brand development and creative direction.

• Ability to translate business goals into creative concepts.

• Strong communication and leadership skills.

• Portfolio of social media or content work.



Nice to Have

• Experience working with service-based businesses.

• Experience directing short-form video content.

• Familiarity with social media performance metrics and engagement strategies.



Work Schedule

This is a remote role with up to 40 hours per week, depending on workload and project needs.

Schedule: Monday to Friday, U.S. Central Standard Time (CST).

Applicants should be comfortable working within this schedule.



Application Requirements

• Link to your updated CV.

• Portfolio or examples of social media content or campaigns you have worked on.

• Creative Exercise: Write three example hooks for a social media post promoting a home listing.

• Start Availability: When would you be available to start?

• Salary Expectations: Monthly salary expectations for a role of up to 40 hours per week.

• Platform Experience: Indicate which platforms you have worked with 
, Instagram,

YouTube, LinkedIn).

*****E-mail applications to: 



Applications without a CV, portfolio, and answers to the questions above will not be reviewed.
